channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: Demonstrators have gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. to protest the automaker’s billionaire leader, Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rodrique Ngowi And Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the US-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the U.S.-Mexico border as President Donald Trump seeks to clamp down on illegal immigration and fulfill a central promise of his campaign, U.S. officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Renowned geneticist Francis Collins retires from NIH, urging 'respect' for embattled workers
item.description: Dr. Francis Collins, a renowned geneticist, and former longtime director of the National Institutes of Health, is retiring from the government agency and urging “respect” for its embattled workers.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: Elon Musk has repeatedly defended federal workforce cuts by his Department of Government Efficiency as “common sense.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Zelenskyy moves on after Oval Office blowout with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has thanked the American people and leadership and voiced hope for “strong relations,” a day after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with U.S. President Donald Trump that left many uncertain where the once staunch allies stood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump makes U.S. copper mining a focus of his domestic minerals policy
item.description: It could mean a new day for U.S. copper mining, and new worries for environmental groups that are contesting proposals such as the stalled Twin Metals project near northern Minnesota's Boundary Waters, a lake-filled wilderness on the U.S.-Canada border.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What's the point of all these anti-DOGE lawsuits? Fight Trump's agenda to SCOTUS, legal experts say
item.description: Legal experts point out that as anti-Trump plaintiffs seek to stifle his agenda, courts are navigating uncharted waters when it comes to DOGE spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved Ukraine closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders support Ukraine amid tensions with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: VP Vance shares special message to ailing Pope Francis amid breathing crisis
item.description: JD Vance shared that though he was “surprised" by Pope Francis’ criticism of President Trump’s immigration policy, he believes the pontiff is “fundamentally a person who cares about the flock of Christians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AG Pam Bondi demands 'full' Epstein files from FBI, vows investigation
item.description: The DOJ released hundreds of Epstein-related files Thursday to a small group of conservative influencers, but Bondi says there's still more to be uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Behold the beaver! Drive is on to make prehistoric giant Minnesota’s official fossil
item.description: Minnesota is among a handful of states that doesn’t have an official fossil among its symbols. The Minnesota Science Museum is hoping to change that.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto slams Trump administration cuts amid bird flu response
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto criticized the Trump administration Thursday, saying recent terminations are worsening the federal government’s response to the bird flu outbreak and the rise price of eggs.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Over 700 National Park Service employees are taking buyouts: reports
item.description: Earlier this month, the Trump administration fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada: Here's when
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting next Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal freeze on energy and climate funding causing concern, confusion in Minnesota
item.description: Some of the money has been released since federal judges ordered the funding to be unfrozen. But weatherization programs and rebates for home efficiency upgrades are still on hold. State and local officials say a lack of communication has led to confusion and uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump shifts date for Canada and Mexico tariffs
item.description: Little about how Trump discusses tariffs is normal — not only because he threatens tariffs on a weekly, even daily, basis, but also because it's often unclear if or when those tariffs will happen.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP team up on bills to battle absenteeism in Minnesota schools
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers this week are moving forward on five bills aimed at addressing chronic absenteeism in Minnesota schools, and DFLers and Republicans are working together on policy solutions.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: National parks already feel the effects of layoffs, even before the busy season starts
item.description: Some 1,000 NPS employees were fired, and hiring for seasonal positions was delayed. Here’s what to know about the impacts already being felt at parks, and what it could mean for the busy season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
